title: NOC 73100 – Concrete finishers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73100 professionals are the foundational experts of Canada’s construction sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, Concrete Finishers finish the surfaces of freshly poured concrete, apply curing or hardening agents, and restore damaged concrete structures. In 2026, as Canada executes its "Green Building Strategy" emphasizing carbon-neutral infrastructure, these workers are classified as Critical Infrastructure Talent. For immigration, this role is a premier candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and Category-Based Selection in Express Entry.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group731 – Masonry and plastering tradesMinor Group7310 – Masonry and plastering tradesNOC Code73100

duties: Formwork Verification: Check formwork, granular base, and steel reinforcement materials to ensure site readiness before pouring.
Placement Direction: Direct the placement of concrete into forms or onto surfaces according to precise grade specifications.
Surface Leveling: Fill hollows and remove high spots to smooth freshly poured concrete using straightedges or floats.
Compaction: Operate power vibrators to compact concrete and ensure no air pockets remain in the structural elements.
Grade Specification: Level the top surface of concrete according to specific grade and depth requirements provided in blueprints.
Manual Finishing: Impart desired finishes to concrete surfaces (smooth, broom, or exposed aggregate) using specialized hand tools.
Power Tool Operation: Utilize power trowels and other mechanical finishers for large-scale industrial flooring and parking deck projects.
Fixture Installation: Install anchor bolts, steel plates, door sills, and other essential fixtures into freshly poured concrete.
Curing & Sealing: Apply hardening, curing, and sealing compounds to protect, stabilize, and waterproof the concrete surface.
Restoration & Waterproofing: Perform waterproofing and damp-proofing; restore, repair, or replace worn and damaged sections of roads, floors, and walls.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a two-to-four-year apprenticeship program is the standard route to qualification.
Experience: Alternatively, over three years of work experience in the trade and some industry-certified courses may be accepted.
Trade Certification:Compulsory in Quebec.Available but voluntary in ON, AB, BC, MB, NB, NS, PEI, and NL.
Available but voluntary in ON, AB, BC, MB, NB, NS, PEI, and NL.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for all immigrants to ensure national recognition and maximum points for PR through the Federal Skilled Trades Program.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced Concrete Finishers on specialized industrial projects or unionized sites often earn annual salaries between $85,000 and $115,000 inclusive of overtime and project premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the sector will face a significant wave of retirements over the next decade. As Canada integrates millions of new housing units and expands its high-frequency rail corridors, the demand for technicians capable of executing precision concrete work ensures long-term job security through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for concrete finishers in 2026 is Very Good to High. In Ontario and British Columbia, the surge in high-rise residential projects and heavy civil engineering (transit and bridge expansions) has created a significant labor deficit. According to the 2026 BuildForce Canada report, the industry is seeing a 12% vacancy rate in urban centers, making it an ideal time for experienced international finishers to secure LMIA-supported job offers to boost their CRS scores.
High-Tech Construction: Work with the latest laser-guided screeds, power trowels, and high-performance, eco-friendly concrete mixes.
Top-Tier Wages: Enjoy competitive hourly rates and significant overtime opportunities on major commercial and industrial sites.
Red Seal Recognition: This is a Red Seal trade, meaning once certified, you have the "Gold Standard" of credentials to work anywhere in Canada.
Strategic PR Pathway: This role is a priority for the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws, often requiring lower CRS scores for an Invitation to Apply (ITA).
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A dedicated route for skilled tradespeople with 2 years of experience and a job offer or certificate of qualification.
OINP Skilled Trades Stream: Ideal for those working in Ontario to secure a provincial nomination.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: Priority for finishers supporting the province’s massive hydroelectric and infrastructure expansion.
